title: Virtuoso performance issue when hosting Freebase
description:
I have set up a virtuoso server for hosting Freebase data, and encountered performance issues.
Let's consider this scenario: find the largest location (probably a county, denoted by ?var1) in Wisconsin State (m.0824r), where ?var1 contains at least one location (denoted by ?var2) with the type "place_with_neighborhoods".
I wrote the SPARQL query as follows:
PREFIX fb: <http://rdf.freebase.com/ns/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?var1 ?var2 ?v2_name WHERE {
  fb:m.0824r fb:location.location.contains ?var1 .
  ?var1 fb:location.location.contains ?var2 .
  ?var2 fb:type.object.type fb:location.place_with_neighborhoods .
  ?var1 fb:location.location.area ?area .
  OPTIONAL {?var2 fb:type.object.name ?v2_name} .
} ORDER BY DESC(?area) LIMIT 1

Unfortunately, the virtuoso engine fail to return the query result for more than one hour.
I tried some simpler queries, which could produce results in less than one second:
PREFIX fb: <http://rdf.freebase.com/ns/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?var1 ?var2 ?v2_name WHERE {
  fb:m.0824r fb:location.location.contains ?var1 .
  ?var1 fb:location.location.contains ?var2 .
  ?var2 fb:type.object.type fb:location.place_with_neighborhoods .
  OPTIONAL {?var2 fb:type.object.name ?v2_name} .
}
# Remove the area-related information with ?var1
# Returns ONLY ONE result in 0.05s.

and,
PREFIX fb: <http://rdf.freebase.com/ns/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?var1 ?var2 ?v2_name ?area WHERE {
  fb:m.0824r fb:location.location.contains ?var1 .
  ?var1 fb:location.location.contains ?var2 .
  ?var1 fb:location.location.area ?area .
  OPTIONAL {?var2 fb:type.object.name ?v2_name} .
}
# Remove the type limitation of ?var2
# Returns ~7000 results in ~1s.

Given the results of those simpler queries, I'm really confused which step brought the performance issue. Is there anybody who can give me some advice? Thank you so much!
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE locations (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), area FLOAT, type VARCHAR(100), neighborhood_id INT);
INSERT INTO locations (name, area, type, neighborhood_id) VALUES ('County A', 500.0, 'place_with_neighborhoods', NULL);
INSERT INTO locations (name, area, type, neighborhood_id) VALUES ('County B', 300.0, 'place_with_neighborhoods', 1);
SELECT l1.name, l2.name, l1.area FROM locations l1 JOIN locations l2 ON l1.id = l2.neighborhood_id WHERE l1.type = 'place_with_neighborhoods' ORDER BY l1.area DESC LIMIT 1;
SELECT name, area FROM locations WHERE area IS NOT NULL AND area > 250.0 ORDER BY area ASC;
key_bug_features: Querying area-related information causes performance issues
